name: Butter Chicken
description: Rich and creamy tomato-based curry with tender chicken pieces, slow-cooked in aromatic spices and finished with butter and cream.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, half the spices and salt for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a heavy pan over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken and cook until golden brown, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter and saute onions until golden. Add ginger garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree and remaining spices. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 10 to 12 minutes until oil separates.
Add the cooked chicken back to the pan. Pour in cream and mix well. Simmer for 8 to 10 minutes.
Finish with kasuri methi crushed between palms. Adjust seasoning. Garnish with fresh coriander and a swirl of cream.

name: Dal Makhani
description: The king of all dals — slow-cooked whole black lentils with rajma, simmered overnight with butter and cream for an indulgently rich flavor.
steps: Wash and soak urad dal and rajma overnight in plenty of water. Drain before cooking.
Pressure cook soaked dal and rajma with salt and water for 20 to 25 minutes until completely soft.
In a heavy pot, heat butter and oil. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add onions and cook until deep golden brown.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook for 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomato puree and all spices. Cook until butter separates, about 10 minutes.
Add the cooked dal to the masala. Mash some lentils with back of spoon for creaminess.
Simmer on very low heat for at least 1 hour, stirring frequently. Add cream, mix well, and simmer another 15 minutes.
Finish with a generous knob of butter. Serve hot garnished with cream and coriander.

name: Palak Paneer
description: Vibrant emerald-green spinach gravy with golden-fried cottage cheese cubes — a nutritious protein-rich dish beloved across India.
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling salted water for 2 minutes. Immediately transfer to ice cold water to retain green color. Drain and blend smooth.
Lightly pan fry paneer cubes in a little oil until golden on edges. Set aside.
In the same pan, heat oil. Add cumin seeds. Once spluttering, add onions and cook until golden.
Add ginger garlic paste and green chilies. Cook for 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add dry spices and cook for a minute. Pour in the spinach puree, mix well, and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Add paneer cubes, stir gently, and simmer 3 to 4 minutes. Finish with cream and garam masala.

name: Punjabi Chole Masala
description: Tangy and deeply spiced chickpea curry infused with robust whole black cardamom, bay leaves, and dried mango powder.
steps: Pressure cook chickpeas with water and salt until tender (20 minutes). Save the broth.
Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, bay leaves, and black cardamom.
Saute onions until deep golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and saute for 2 minutes.
Add tomato puree and cook until the oil starts separating from the masala.
Stir in chole masala, amchur powder, salt, and the cooked chickpeas with their liquid.
Simmer for 15 minutes, mashing a few chickpeas with the spoon to thicken the curry gravy.

name: Shahi Paneer
description: A royal preparation of cottage cheese cubes simmered in a silky, rich gravy of cashew nuts, cream, and sweet aromatic spices.
steps: Boil onions, tomatoes, and cashew nuts in 1 cup of water for 10 minutes. Cool and blend into a super smooth paste.
Heat ghee in a pan, add ginger-garlic paste and saute for a minute.
Pour in the blended cashew-tomato-onion paste and cook for 5 to 7 minutes.
Add red chili powder, cardamom powder, and salt. Stir well.
Add paneer cubes and half a cup of water. Simmer covered for 5 minutes.
Gently stir in the fresh heavy cream and turn off the heat before serving.

name: Rajma Masala
description: Comfort food at its finest — tender red kidney beans slow-cooked in a thick, classic onion-tomato gravy packed with Punjabi spices.
ingredients: 1.5 cups red kidney beans, soaked overnight
steps: Pressure cook the soaked kidney beans with salt and water until soft (about 25 minutes).
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot and splutter cumin seeds. Add onions and saute till brown.
Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in the fresh tomato puree.
Add coriander powder, turmeric, red chili powder, and cook until oil splits from the base.
Add the boiled rajma along with its cooking water. Stir well and mash a few beans against the sides.
Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes until the gravy turns rich and thick. Stir in garam masala.

name: Aloo Gobi Matar
description: A classic dry North Indian sabzi made with fresh cauliflower florets, diced potatoes, and green peas tossed with turmeric and ginger.
steps: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. Add chopped onions and saute until transparent.
Add ginger juliennes and green chilies. Stir for a minute.
Add potatoes and cauliflower florets. Stir-fry on medium-high heat for 5 minutes.
Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix thoroughly to coat the vegetables.
Add green peas, cover the pan with a lid, and cook on low heat for 15 minutes until tender.
Uncover and cook for an extra 2 minutes to crisp the edges slightly. Garnish with coriander.

name: Kadai Paneer
description: Cottage cheese cubes and crunchy bell peppers cooked in a spicy, freshly ground coriander and red chili spice mix.
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds and dry red chilies. Grind into a coarse powder (Kadai Masala).
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai. Saute onion petals and capsicum cubes for 3 minutes until crisp-tender. Remove.
Add remaining oil to the same pan. Saute ginger-garlic paste, then add tomato puree.
Cook the tomatoes for 8 minutes. Add the ground kadai masala and salt.
Add the paneer cubes, sauteed onions, and capsicum. Mix gently to avoid breaking paneer.
Simmer for 5 minutes, sprinkle crushed kasuri methi, and serve hot.

name: Malai Kofta
description: Deep-fried potato and paneer dumplings served swimming in an incredibly smooth, sweet, and creamy tomato-cashew sauce.
steps: Mix paneer, mashed potatoes, cornflour, and salt. Knead into a smooth dough.
Form small balls, stuffing the center of each with a couple of cashews and raisins.
Deep fry the balls (koftas) in hot oil on medium heat until beautiful golden brown. Set aside.
In another pan, heat the gravy base, stir in kitchen king masala, cream, and salt.
Simmer the gravy for 5 minutes until completely warmed through and silky.
Place hot koftas in a serving dish, pour the warm creamy gravy over them right before serving.

name: Baingan Bharta
description: Smoky fire-roasted eggplant mashed and stir-fried with pungent mustard oil, garlic, green chilies, onions, and tomatoes.
steps: Make slits in the eggplant and insert a few garlic cloves. Roast directly over an open gas flame until charred and completely collapsed.
Let it cool, peel away the burnt skin, and mash the inner smoky flesh completely.
Heat mustard oil in a pan until smoking. Saute remaining onions, garlic, and green chilies.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
Add turmeric, chili powder, salt, and the mashed roasted eggplant flesh.
Cook on medium heat for 10 minutes while stirring constantly to seal the rich smoky flavor.

name: Matar Paneer
description: An everyday North Indian staple featuring tender green peas and soft cottage cheese cubes cooked in a spiced homestyle gravy.
steps: Heat oil in a pan and lightly pan-fry paneer cubes until faint golden. Remove and soak in warm water.
In the same oil, add onion puree and saute until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Pour in tomato puree.
Add coriander powder, garam masala, and salt. Cook until oil separates.
Add green peas and drained paneer cubes along with 1 cup of water.
Cover and simmer for 10 minutes on low heat until the peas are tender and gravy is uniform.

name: Bhindi Do Pyaza
description: Crisp okra stir-fried with double the quantity of onions added at two different stages for a sweet and savory crunch.
ingredients: 400g okra (bhindi), wiped dry and cut into 1-inch pieces
2 large onions (1 finely chopped, 1 cut into large cubes)
steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Saute the okra pieces on medium heat for 8 minutes until non-sticky. Remove.
In the same pan, saute the large onion cubes for 2 minutes until translucent but crunchy. Remove.
Add remaining oil, splutter carom seeds, and saute the finely chopped onions until golden.
Add tomatoes, chili powder, amchur, and salt. Cook until soft.
Toss back the cooked okra and onion cubes into the pan.
Mix well and cook uncovered on low heat for 5 minutes until fully seasoned.

name: Jeera Aloo
description: The quickest Indian side dish — boiled potatoes tossed in a generous amount of toasted cumin seeds, turmeric, and fresh coriander.
ingredients: 4 large potatoes, boiled, peeled, and diced
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a pan on medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them completely crackle and turn aromatic.
Add chopped green chili and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add the diced boiled potatoes to the pan.
Sprinkle turmeric powder, amchur powder, and salt over the potatoes.
Toss gently on medium-high heat for 5 to 7 minutes until potatoes develop a light golden crust.
Garnish with lots of chopped coriander leaves and serve.

name: Dal Tadka
description: Comforting yellow pigeon pea lentils cooked soft, topped with a sizzling tempering of ghee, garlic, cumin, and dry red chilies.
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with onion, tomato, turmeric, salt, and 3 cups of water for 4 whistles.
Whisk the cooked dal lightly until uniform and smooth.
For the tadka, heat ghee in a small small tempering pan.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add sliced garlic and fry until dark golden.
Turn off flame, drop in the dry red chilies and a pinch of red chili powder.
Pour this sizzling aromatic tempering instantly into the hot dal and cover with a lid for 2 minutes.

name: Kashmiri Dum Aloo
description: Baby potatoes poked, deep fried, and slow-cooked under pressure ('dum') in a spicy yogurt sauce enriched with fennel and ginger powder.
steps: Boil baby potatoes until 80 percent done. Peel and prick them all over with a toothpick.
Deep fry or shallow fry the potatoes in hot oil until a crisp golden crust forms. Set aside.
In a bowl, mix whisked yogurt with fennel powder, ginger powder, and chili powder.
Heat mustard oil, add whole spices, then reduce heat and pour in the spiced yogurt mix.
Stir constantly to prevent yogurt curdling. Add fried potatoes and half a cup of water.
Seal the pan with a tight lid and simmer on very low heat for 15 minutes until gravy is absorbed.

name: Mixed Vegetable Korma
description: An assortment of seasonal vegetables simmered beautifully in a delicately balanced coconut and cashew nut sauce.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed chopped vegetables (carrots, beans, peas, corn)
steps: Blend grated coconut, cashews, and poppy seeds with a little water into a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a pan, saute chopped onions until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute.
Add the chopped mixed vegetables and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
Pour in 1 cup of water, cover, and cook until the vegetables are 90 percent soft.
Stir in the blended coconut-cashew paste and salt. Simmer gently uncovered for 5 minutes.

name: Methi Matar Malai
description: A classic rich curry balancing the subtle bitterness of fresh fenugreek leaves with sweet green peas and heavy white cream.
steps: Sprinkle salt over chopped methi leaves, let sit for 10 minutes, then squeeze out bitter water.
Heat ghee in a pan and saute the boiled onion paste and green chili paste for 3 minutes.
Add the cashew paste and cook until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan.
Add the methi leaves and green peas, stir-frying everything together for 5 minutes.
Add half a cup of water and bring to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Lower the flame, stir in the heavy cream completely, and remove from heat.

name: Sarson Ka Saag
description: Traditional Punjabi winter greens dish made from fresh mustard leaves and spinach, slow-churned with maize flour and topped with white butter.
steps: Wash greens thoroughly. Clean, chop, and pressure cook them with ginger, garlic, green chilies, and salt for 15 minutes.
Blend roughly or mash traditionally using a wooden churner.
Add maize flour to the mashed greens, mix well to avoid lumps, and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.
In a separate small pan, heat ghee and saute a bit of extra garlic and onions until brown.
Pour this hot tempering directly onto the simmering saag.
Mix well and serve piping hot with a dollop of fresh butter on top.

name: Pindi Chana
description: A dry, dark-colored chickpea dish from Rawalpindi, loaded with robust roasted dry spices and pomegranate seed powder.
ingredients: 1.5 tbsp dry pomegranate seeds (anardana), roasted and ground
1 tbsp coriander seeds, roasted and ground
steps: Pressure cook chickpeas with water, salt, and tea bags until tender. The tea bags give them a distinct dark color. Drain.
In a bowl, mix the ground coriander seeds, ground anardana, garam masala, and chili powder.
Toss the dry ground spice mix over the hot boiled chickpeas to coat them evenly.
Heat oil in a pan, add slit green chilies and ginger juliennes.
Add the coated chickpeas to the pan and stir-fry on low heat for 10 minutes.
Crush kasuri methi over the top and serve hot without any wet gravy.

name: Paneer Bhurji
description: Scrambled fresh cottage cheese cooked rapidly with finely chopped onions, tomatoes, green chilies, and basic kitchen spices.
steps: Heat oil or melt butter in a wide frying pan over medium flame.
Add chopped onions and green chilies. Saute until onions turn soft and translucent.
Add tomatoes and cook for 3 minutes until they begin to break down.
Stir in turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt.
Add the crumbled fresh paneer to the mixture. Stir-fry rapidly for 3 to 4 minutes.
Do not overcook as paneer will turn rubbery. Turn off heat and garnish with coriander.

name: Masala Dosa
description: Crispy golden crepe made from fermented rice lentil batter, filled with spiced potato filling, served with coconut chutney and sambar.
steps: For filling, heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, and onions. Saute until soft.
Add turmeric, salt, and mashed potatoes. Mix well and set aside.
Heat a flat non-stick tawa, spread a ladle of batter thinly in a circular motion.
Drizzle oil around edges and cook until golden brown and crisp.
Place a portion of potato filling in the center, fold over, and serve hot.

name: Hotel Sambar
description: A tangy lentil stew loaded with mixed vegetables like drumsticks and pumpkin, flavored with a unique freshly ground spice paste.
ingredients: 1 cup mixed chopped vegetables (drumstick, pumpkin, carrot)
steps: Pressure cook toor dal until completely smooth and mash it.
Soak tamarind in warm water and extract the thick juice.
Boil the vegetables in water along with sambar powder, tamarind extract, and salt until cooked.
Add the mashed dal to the boiling vegetables and simmer for 5 minutes.
Prepare a tempering of mustard seeds and red chilies in ghee, pour over the sambar.

name: Soft Steamed Idli
description: Fluffy, pillow-soft savory cakes made from a fermented batter of rice and dehulled black lentils, steamed to perfection.
steps: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek seeds separately for 5 hours.
Grind urad dal to a fluffy paste and rice to a slightly coarse texture. Mix together with salt.
Let the batter ferment in a warm place overnight (8 to 12 hours) until it doubles in volume.
Grease idli molds, pour batter into each slot carefully.
Steam in an idli cooker or closed steamer on medium heat for exactly 10 to 12 minutes.

name: Medu Vada
description: Crispy on the outside, light and soft inside doughnut-shaped fritters made from spiced black lentil batter.
steps: Grind the soaked urad dal using very minimal ice water into a thick, airy paste.
Whip the batter vigorously with your hands to incorporate air until it passes a water float test.
Mix in green chili, peppercorns, curry leaves, and salt.
Wet your palms, take a small ball of batter, shape it, and make a hole in the center.
Slide gently into smoking hot oil and deep fry until crispy and golden brown.

name: Lemon Rice
description: A vibrant and comforting rice dish tempered with crunchy peanuts, curry leaves, turmeric, and fresh lemon juice.
steps: Heat oil in a wok, add mustard seeds, chana dal, and peanuts. Fry until golden.
Add curry leaves, slit green chilies, and turmeric powder. Turn off the flame.
Pour fresh lemon juice directly into the warm tempering pan.
Add the cooled cooked rice and salt to the pan.
Mix gently with a spatula from the sides to ensure the rice grains do not break.

name: Mumbai Vada Pav
description: The quintessential street food of Mumbai — deep-fried spiced potato dumpling stuffed inside a soft bread bun lined with fiery garlic chutney.
steps: Temper mustard seeds, garlic-chili paste, and turmeric. Mix directly into mashed potatoes with salt.
Shape the potato mixture into round balls.
Make a smooth batter with besan, water, salt, and a pinch of turmeric.
Dip potato balls into the besan batter and deep fry in hot oil until crisp.
Slice pav buns, apply dry garlic chutney, insert hot potato vada, and press gently.

name: Pav Bhaji
description: A thick mash of mixed vegetables cooked with a special spice blend on a flat griddle, served with a massive dollop of butter and toasted pav buns.
ingredients: 2 cups mixed boiled vegetables (potatoes, cauliflower, peas)
steps: Mash the boiled vegetables thoroughly into a smooth paste using a potato masher.
Melt 2 tbsp butter in a pan, saute onions and ginger-garlic paste for 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Stir in pav bhaji masala and salt.
Add the mashed vegetables along with half a cup of water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Toast pav buns on a pan with extra butter. Serve with raw onions and lemon wedges.

name: Pani Puri
description: Hollow crispy fried puris filled with a refreshing, tangy, and spicy mint-coriander water along with boiled potatoes and black chickpeas.
steps: Blend mint leaves, coriander leaves, green chilies, and tamarind with water to make a thin mixture.
Strain the water, add pani puri masala and black salt, then chill inside the refrigerator.
Mix mashed potatoes, black chickpeas, and regular salt together for the stuffing.
Poke a hole carefully in the center of a crisp puri.
Stuff with potato mixture, dip completely into the chilled tangy water, and eat immediately.

name: Crispy Punjabi Samosa
description: Flaky pyramid pastry crusts stuffed to the brim with a heavily spiced mixture of green peas and mashed potatoes.
ingredients: 4 tbsp ghee (for the dough flaky texture)
steps: Rub ghee into maida with salt, add stiff water slowly, and knead into a very firm dough. Rest 30 minutes.
Saute crushed coriander seeds, ginger, green peas, potatoes, and dry spices for 5 minutes. Let cool.
Roll dough into an oval shape, cut in half, shape each half into a hollow cone.
Fill the cone with the potato mixture, seal edges carefully using a tiny bit of water.
Deep fry very slowly on low heat for 15 minutes until pastry turns light golden and perfectly crispy.

name: Bhel Puri
description: A light and breezy beachside snack made from crunchy puffed rice, raw onions, tomatoes, and dynamic sweet and sour chutneys.
steps: Dry roast puffed rice for 2 minutes in a pan so it turns incredibly crunchy. Let it cool.
In a wide bowl, combine puffed rice, chopped onions, and chopped tomatoes.
Sprinkle chaat masala and salt evenly over the dry mix.
Drizzle the spicy green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney over everything.
Mix rapidly with a large spoon and serve immediately topped with a handful of nylon sev.

name: Nylon Khaman Dhokla
description: Spongy, juicy, and sweet-sour steamed savory cakes made from gram flour, tempered beautifully with mustard seeds and green chilies.
ingredients: 1 tsp citric acid or lemon juice
steps: Whisk besan, water, lemon juice, oil, and salt into a smooth, lump-free batter.
Add fruit salt, stir gently in one direction until batter turns frothy and light.
Pour instantly into a greased pan and steam over high heat for 15 minutes.
Make a tempering by heating oil, adding mustard seeds, green chilies, sugar, and half a cup of water.
Cut the steamed dhokla into cubes and pour the warm sweet tempering water evenly over them.

name: Methi Thepla
description: Ultra-thin, soft flatbreads made with whole wheat flour, fresh fenugreek leaves, yogurt, and aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1 cup fresh methi leaves, finely chopped
steps: Combine flour, chopped methi, curd, turmeric, sesame seeds, salt, and 1 tbsp oil in a large bowl.
Knead into a soft dough using small amounts of water. Rest for 10 minutes.
Divide into small balls and roll out into very thin circles.
Heat a tawa on high heat, place the rolled flatbread, and cook for 30 seconds.
Flip, apply half a teaspoon of oil, and roast quickly pressing down until golden brown spots appear.

name: Surti Undhiyu
description: A winter delicacy from Surat — a rich mixed vegetable casserole made with broad beans, sweet potatoes, purple yam, and fenugreek dumplings.
ingredients: 1 cup mixed root vegetables (purple yam, sweet potato, potato)
half cup grated coconut & peanut paste
steps: Deep fry the diced root vegetables until cooked through. Prepare fried methi muthias separately.
Stuff the vegetables with a rich paste made of grated coconut, ground peanuts, green chilies, and coriander.
Heat oil in a pressure cooker or deep pan, add mustard seeds and flat green beans.
Layer the stuffed vegetables over the beans carefully. Add fried muthias on the very top.
Add a splash of water, seal, and slow cook on low heat for 20 minutes until thoroughly integrated.

name: Baked Vegetable Handvo
description: A savory cake made from a fermented batter of lentils, rice, and grated bottle gourd, baked crisp with a sesame-mustard tempering.
ingredients: 2 cups handvo flour (mixed rice and lentil mix)
steps: Mix handvo flour with curd and water to form a thick batter. Ferment overnight for 8 hours.
Add grated bottle gourd, turmeric powder, salt, and a tablespoon of oil to the fermented batter.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a heavy iron pan, add mustard seeds and sesame seeds until they crackle.
Pour a thick layer of batter directly into the pan over the tempering seeds.
Cover with a heavy lid and cook on low heat for 15 minutes on each side until a deep brown crust forms.

name: Sev Tamatar Nu Shaak
description: A quick and tangy sweet-and-spicy Gujarati tomato curry topped with a generous handful of thick gram flour sev noodles.
steps: Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Saute onions until soft.
Add chopped tomatoes, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt.
Cook on medium heat for 7 minutes until the tomatoes turn completely mushy.
Add jaggery and half a cup of water, bring the curry gravy to a boil.
Just 2 minutes before serving, drop the thick sev into the boiling gravy and turn off heat immediately.

name: Kesar Pista Shrikhand
description: A creamy dessert made from thick strained yogurt, whipped with powdered sugar, fragrant saffron strands, and crushed pistachios.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh curd (to make hung curd)
steps: Tie curd in a cheesecloth and hang it over a sink for 4 hours to drain out all whey water completely.
Soak saffron strands in one tablespoon of warm milk for 10 minutes.
Transfer the thick resulting hung curd (chakka) into a wide glass bowl.
Add powdered sugar and saffron milk. Whisk vigorously until smooth and completely creamy.
Fold in cardamom powder and half the pistachios. Chill inside the refrigerator for 2 hours before serving.

name: Rice Kheer
description: Slow-cooked rice pudding simmered in full-cream milk until thick, sweetened with sugar and laced with almonds and green cardamom.
ingredients: quarter cup basmati rice, washed and soaked 20 minutes
steps: Bring full cream milk to a boil in a heavy-bottomed pan over a medium flame.
Drain rice and add it to the boiling milk. Lower heat immediately.
Simmer for 25 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice grains are completely soft and milk reduces.
Add sugar and cardamom powder. Simmer for an additional 5 minutes until sugar dissolves completely.
Garnish with sliced almonds and cashews. Serve either warm or thoroughly chilled.

name: Gajar Ka Halwa
description: The quintessential winter dessert made by slow-cooking sweet red grated carrots in milk, ghee, and sugar, finished with roasted nuts.
ingredients: 1 kg red carrots, washed and finely grated
steps: Combine grated carrots and milk in a deep heavy pan. Cook on medium heat until all milk evaporates.
Add ghee to the pan and saute the carrots continuously for 10 minutes until they change color.
Stir in the sugar and continue cooking. The sugar will release moisture and turn liquid.
Cook until the mixture dries up again. Stir in crumbled khoya and cook for 3 minutes.
Garnish generously with cashews fried in a little extra ghee.

name: Spongy Bengali Rasgulla
description: Soft and spongy white chenna cottage cheese dumplings boiled and soaked inside a light, clear sugar syrup.
steps: Boil milk, add lemon juice to curdle it completely. Strain through a cloth to collect fresh chenna.
Rinse chenna under cold tap water to remove lemon taste, hang for 45 minutes to drain.
Knead chenna thoroughly with the heel of your palm for 10 minutes until it turns oily and smooth.
Roll into small, smooth, crack-free balls.
Boil sugar and water together, drop chenna balls in, cover tightly, and boil on high heat for 15 minutes.

name: Gulab Jamun
description: Melt in the mouth deep fried milk solid dumplings soaked in rose cardamom syrup — the most beloved Indian sweet for every celebration.
ingredients: quarter cup all purpose flour maida
steps: Make sugar syrup by boiling sugar, water, and cardamom until sugar dissolves, then simmer 5 minutes.
Mix khoya, flour, and baking soda gently with fingertips. Add milk a little at a time to form a soft dough.
Divide and roll into smooth, completely crack-free balls.
Fry jamuns slowly on low heat, turning continuously for an even dark golden color.
Transfer hot jamuns straight into the warm sugar syrup and let soak for 2 hours.

name: Bengali Aloo Posto
description: A comforting Bengali classic consisting of diced potatoes cooked simply in a thick, nutty paste of ground poppy seeds.
steps: Soak poppy seeds in warm water for 20 minutes, then grind with green chilies into a thick paste.
Heat mustard oil in a pan until smoking, temper with nigella seeds.
Add potato cubes and stir-fry on medium heat for 5 minutes.
Pour in the ground poppy seed paste along with half a cup of water and salt.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the gravy dries up.

name: Begun Bhaja
description: Thick discs of aubergine marinated with turmeric, salt, and a touch of sugar, pan-fried crisp inside aromatic mustard oil.
ingredients: 1 large round eggplant, cut into half-inch thick slices
steps: Rub eggplant slices evenly on both sides with turmeric, chili powder, sugar, and salt.
Let the seasoned slices marinate for 10 minutes so they absorb flavors.
Heat mustard oil in a wide skillet until it starts smoking gently.
Place eggplant slices in a single layer and fry on medium heat for 5 minutes.
Flip carefully and fry the other side until both sides turn completely dark golden and tender.

name: Vegetable Pulao
description: A fragrant one-pot rice dish loaded with fresh garden vegetables and scented with whole cinnamon, cloves, and bay leaves.
ingredients: Whole spices (1 bay leaf, 1 cinnamon stick, 3 cloves)
steps: Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add whole spices and saute until aromatic.
Add sliced onions and fry until light golden brown.
Add mixed vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the drained basmati rice and stir gently to coat grains with ghee.
Add 3 cups of water and salt, close lid, and cook for exactly 2 whistles on medium flame.

name: Moong Dal Khichdi
description: The ultimate Indian comfort food — a wholesome and soothing porridge made of rice and yellow lentils tempered lightly with cumin and ghee.
steps: Wash rice and moong dal together thoroughly, soak in water for 15 minutes. Drain.
Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and asafoetida.
Add the drained rice and dal mixture, turmeric powder, and salt. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Pour in 4.5 cups of water (extra water ensures a porridge-like soft consistency).
Close the cooker lid and cook on medium heat for 4 whistles before turning off gas.

name: Bisi Bele Bhath
description: A hot, tangy, and flavorful lentil-rice mash from Karnataka cooked with mixed vegetables, tamarind, and a special spice blend.
steps: Pressure cook rice, toor dal, and vegetables together with 4 cups of water until completely soft.
In a deep pot, add the cooked rice-dal mash, tamarind extract, and bisi bele bhath powder.
Mix thoroughly, add salt and a cup of water, and let it simmer for 10 minutes on low heat.
Prepare a tempering of mustard seeds, cashew nuts, and curry leaves in hot ghee.
Pour the hot tempering directly onto the rice dish, mix well, and serve warm.

name: Hyderabadi Dum Biryani
description: The legendary layered rice dish — fragrant basmati rice and marinated chicken slow-cooked in a sealed pot with saffron, caramelized onions, and whole spices.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, chili powder, biryani masala, and half the fried onions for 2 hours.
Parboil basmati rice with whole spices until 70 percent cooked. Drain immediately.
Layer marinated chicken at the bottom of a heavy pot, top with parboiled rice.
Drizzle ghee, saffron milk, remaining fried onions, mint, and coriander leaves over rice.
Seal pot tightly with foil and a lid. Cook on low flame for 40 minutes ('dum').

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Roasted marinated chicken chunks (tikkas) tossed inside a spiced, creamy, orange-colored tomato sauce.
steps: Marinate chicken cubes with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, and salt for 1 hour.
Skewers the chicken pieces and pan-sear or grill until charred on the edges (12 minutes).
In another pan, cook tomato puree with remaining spices until oil starts separating.
Stir in heavy cream to create a rich, uniform orange sauce base.
Add the grilled chicken tikka pieces to the sauce and simmer together for 8 minutes.

name: Kashmiri Rogan Josh
description: An aromatic mutton dish from Kashmir colored intensely with alkanet root and flavored with fennel seeds and ginger powder.
ingredients: 2 tbsp kashmiri red chili powder (for color)
steps: Heat mustard oil in a deep heavy pan until smoking. Add mutton pieces and sear for 8 minutes.
Lower heat, add kashmiri chili powder mixed with a little water to prevent burning.
Pour in whisked yogurt slowly while stirring continuously to prevent splitting.
Add fennel powder, ginger powder, and salt. Add 1 cup of water.
Cover tightly and slow-cook on low flame for 1 hour until the mutton is completely fork-tender.

name: Goan Fish Curry
description: A warm, tangy, and fiery fish curry made with freshly ground coconut paste, tamarind, and local Goan spices.
ingredients: 500g firm white fish fillets (Kingfish or Pomfret)
steps: Grind fresh grated coconut, dry red chilies, coriander seeds, and tamarind into a very smooth paste.
In a wide pan, mix the ground paste with coconut milk and 1 cup of water. Bring to a boil.
Add slit green chilies and salt to the boiling curry base.
Gently slide in the clean fish fillets into the pan.
Simmer uncovered on medium-low heat for 8 minutes until the fish is flaky and cooked through.

name: Shahi Chicken Korma
description: Rich chicken dish cooked in a highly aromatic gravy enriched with brown onion paste, yogurt, and cashew nuts.
ingredients: 2 large onions, sliced and fried brown
steps: Blend the crispy fried onions and yogurt into a thick, smooth paste.
Heat ghee in a pot, add chicken pieces, and saute until they turn white.
Stir in the blended onion-yogurt paste, cashew paste, and garam masala.
Add half a cup of water, cover, and simmer over low heat for 25 minutes until chicken is tender.

name: Mango Lassi
description: Cool thick and refreshing blended yogurt drink with ripe Alphonso mangoes and a touch of cardamom — India's favorite summer cooler.
steps: Blend the fresh mango pieces inside a blender until completely smooth.
Add thick yogurt, chilled milk, sugar, and cardamom powder.
Blend again for 1 minute until frothy and uniform.
Pour into tall chilled glasses over ice cubes and serve instantly.

name: Spiced Masala Chaas
description: A refreshing, digestive buttermilk drink spiced with roasted cumin powder, black salt, mint, and green chilies.
steps: Add curd, cold water, mint leaves, green chili, black salt, and cumin powder into a blender.
Churn or blend for 30 seconds until a nice layer of froth forms on the top surface.
Pour into glasses, garnish with a dusting of roasted cumin powder, and serve cold.

name: Cutting Masala Chai
description: Authentic Indian tea brewed robustly with milk, sugar, crushed ginger, green cardamom, and black peppercorns.
steps: Bring water to a rolling boil in a tea pan along with crushed ginger and cardamoms.
Add black tea leaves and simmer for 2 minutes until the water turns dark amber.
Pour in the milk and add sugar. Bring the tea to a boil.
Allow it to rise to the top 2 times, lowering heat in between to brew deeply. Strain into cups.

name: Shahi Shikanji
description: Traditional Indian lemonade elevated with black salt, roasted cumin powder, and fresh mint leaves.
steps: Dissolve sugar completely in a pitcher containing one cup of water first.
Squeeze out fresh lemon juice directly into the sweetened water mix.
Add remaining cold water, black salt, and roasted cumin powder.
Stir thoroughly, add crushed ice cubes, and serve garnished with mint leaves.

name: Kesar Badam Milk
description: A nourishing drink made from almond paste boiled with full cream milk, sweetened with sugar, and tinted with royal saffron.
steps: Grind the peeled soaked almonds with 2 tablespoons of milk into a smooth, fine paste.
Boil milk in a pan, add the fresh almond paste and saffron strands.
Simmer on low flame for 10 minutes, stirring continuously to prevent burning.
Stir in sugar and cardamom powder. Serve either hot or thoroughly chilled.

name: Banarasi Thandai
description: A festive cooling drink packed with a ground paste of almonds, fennel seeds, watermelon seeds, black pepper, and rose petals.
ingredients: quarter cup mixed nuts (almonds, cashews, pistachios)
steps: Soak nuts, fennel seeds, poppy seeds, and black pepper in warm water for 2 hours. Drain.
Grind the soaked ingredients into an incredibly smooth, fine paste.
Mix the ground paste and sugar into the cold milk thoroughly.
Strain the mixture through a fine sieve to remove any residual grit.
Chill inside the refrigerator for 3 hours before serving to let flavors mature.

name: Jal Jeera
description: A tangy, appetizers drink made from fresh mint, coriander, tamarind, and fried chickpea flour droplets (boondi).
steps: Blend mint leaves, coriander leaves, and tamarind paste with a little water until smooth.
Combine the paste with 4 cups of chilled water in a large jug.
Stir in roasted cumin powder, dry mango powder, and black salt.
Chill well, strain, and serve topped with a handful of salty crisp boondi.

name: Punjabi Sweet Lassi
description: Creamy, thick traditional Punjabi churned yogurt beverage sweetened with sugar and finished with malai cream.
steps: Combine thick yogurt, cold water, and sugar inside a deep mixing container.
Use a traditional wooden hand whisker or a modern blender briefly until sugar dissolves.
Do not over-blend or the yogurt will thin out too much. Mix in rose water.
Pour into clay glasses and top with a thick layer of fresh cream before serving.

name: Gulkand Rose Milk
description: A cooling pink summer beverage made with chilled milk, sweet rose syrup, and floral gulkand preserve.
steps: Soak basil seeds (sabja) in water for 10 minutes until they swell up completely.
In a pitcher, whisk chilled milk, sweet rose syrup, and gulkand together thoroughly.
Add a spoonful of the soaked gelatinous basil seeds into individual serving glasses.
Pour the pink rose milk over the seeds, stir gently, and serve super cold.

name: Goan Kokum Sherbet
description: A deep crimson-colored coastal drink made from tangly dried kokum skins, sweetened with sugar and spiked with black salt.
steps: Soak dried kokum petals in 1 cup of hot water for 30 minutes until they completely soften.
Mash the soaked kokum thoroughly with a spoon to extract all its dark sour juices. Strain.
In a jar, mix the sour kokum extract juice with sugar syrup, black salt, and roasted cumin.
Dilute with 3 cups of chilled water, drop in ice cubes, and serve cold.
